title: Svargarohanaparvan - Canto 001
translation_text:
Vaishampayana said: Then King Yudhishthira, worshipped by the gods, the sages, and the hosts of Maruts, went to where those bulls of the Kuru clan were.

There he saw Govinda, endowed with a Brahmanical form, Indicated by the similarity of what had been seen before.

Shining with its own body, approached by divine weapons, By the terrible divine forms of the discus and others, Being worshipped by the heroic Phalguna, of good radiance,

In another place, the descendant of the Kurus saw Karna, the best of warriors, together with the twelve suns.

Then in another place, he saw the lord surrounded by the group of Maruts, Bhīmasena, endowed with the same form.

In the place of the Ashvins, shining with their own radiance, The son of the Kurus saw Nakula and Sahadeva,

And he saw Panchali, wearing garlands of lotuses and lilies, standing with a body shining like the sun, as if she had ascended to heaven.

Then King Yudhishthira suddenly wanted to ask her. Then the Blessed One, the King of the Gods, told him.

She is Shri, in the form of Draupadi, who has come to be a human for your sake. She is born without a womb, beloved of the world, fragrant with merit, O Yudhishthira.

Born in the family of Drupada, she has been supported by you all. For your pleasure, she has been created by the Trident-bearer.

These five fortunate Gandharvas, radiant like fire, are the sons of Draupadi, O king, of immeasurable energy.

Look at the Gandharva king, the wise Dhritarashtra, And know him as your elder brother, the son of your father.

This is your elder brother, the son of Kunti, shining like fire, The eldest and best, the son of Surya, known as Karna, the son of Radha. He is going with the sun. See him, the bull among men.

Among the Sadhyas, the gods, the Vasus, and the Maruts, O king, look at the great chariots of the Vrishnis and Andhakas. Satyaki and other heroes, and the great chariots of the Bhojas,

See Subhadra, the unconquered, together with Soma. Abhimānyu, the great archer, shining like the moon.

This Pandu, a great archer, is united with Kunti and Madri. He always comes to me in a chariot. He is your father.

See Bhishma, the king of the Shantanu lineage, together with the Vasus. Look at Drona, the preceptor, at the side of Brihaspati.

These and other kings, O Pandava, and the warriors, together with the gandharvas, yakshas, and the virtuous, are going.

Some of the best of men have attained the state of the Guhyakas, having abandoned their bodies and conquered heaven by their meritorious speech, intellect, and deeds.
shlokas_raw:
वैशंपायन उवाचततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा देवैः सर्षिमरुद्गणैःपूज्यमानो ययौ तत्र यत्र ते कुरुपुंगवाः

ददर्श तत्र गोविन्दं ब्राह्मेण वपुषान्वितम्तेनैव दृष्टपूर्वेण सादृश्येनोपसूचितम्

दीप्यमानं स्ववपुषा दिव्यैरस्त्रैरुपस्थितम्चक्रप्रभृतिभिर्घोरैर्दिव्यैः पुरुषविग्रहैःउपास्यमानं वीरेण फल्गुनेन सुवर्चसा

अपरस्मिन्नथोद्देशे कर्णं शस्त्रभृतां वरम्द्वादशादित्यसहितं ददर्श कुरुनन्दनः

अथापरस्मिन्नुद्देशे मरुद्गणवृतं प्रभुम्भीमसेनमथापश्यत्तेनैव वपुषान्वितम्

अश्विनोस्तु तथा स्थाने दीप्यमानौ स्वतेजसानकुलं सहदेवं च ददर्श कुरुनन्दनः

तथा ददर्श पाञ्चालीं कमलोत्पलमालिनीम्वपुषा स्वर्गमाक्रम्य तिष्ठन्तीमर्कवर्चसम्

अथैनां सहसा राजा प्रष्टुमैच्छद्युधिष्ठिरःततोऽस्य भगवानिन्द्रः कथयामास देवराट्

श्रीरेषा द्रौपदीरूपा त्वदर्थे मानुषं गताअयोनिजा लोककान्ता पुण्यगन्धा युधिष्ठिर

द्रुपदस्य कुले जाता भवद्भिश्चोपजीवितारत्यर्थं भवतां ह्येषा निर्मिता शूलपाणिना

एते पञ्च महाभागा गन्धर्वाः पावकप्रभाःद्रौपद्यास्तनया राजन्युष्माकममितौजसः

पश्य गन्धर्वराजानं धृतराष्ट्रं मनीषिणम्एनं च त्वं विजानीहि भ्रातरं पूर्वजं पितुः

अयं ते पूर्वजो भ्राता कौन्तेयः पावकद्युतिःसूर्यपुत्रोऽग्रजः श्रेष्ठो राधेय इति विश्रुतःआदित्यसहितो याति पश्यैनं पुरुषर्षभ

साध्यानामथ देवानां वसूनां मरुतामपिगणेषु पश्य राजेन्द्र वृष्ण्यन्धकमहारथान्सात्यकिप्रमुखान्वीरान्भोजांश्चैव महारथान्

सोमेन सहितं पश्य सौभद्रमपराजितम्अभिमन्युं महेष्वासं निशाकरसमद्युतिम्

एष पाण्डुर्महेष्वासः कुन्त्या माद्र्या च संगतःविमानेन सदाभ्येति पिता तव ममान्तिकम्

वसुभिः सहितं पश्य भीष्मं शांतनवं नृपम्द्रोणं बृहस्पतेः पार्श्वे गुरुमेनं निशामय

एते चान्ये महीपाला योधास्तव च पाण्डवगन्धर्वैः सहिता यान्ति यक्षैः पुण्यजनैस्तथा

गुह्यकानां गतिं चापि केचित्प्राप्ता नृसत्तमाःत्यक्त्वा देहं जितस्वर्गाः पुण्यवाग्बुद्धिकर्मभिः
